content_review: NOT_APPLICABLE — no governed SSOT content files touched. All changes are within the upload-review contract seam (frontend components, types, state store). Backend parse route and analysis route read but not modified.
behaviour_review: COMPLETE — MIXED correct. This sprint changes the data entering analysis (user-corrected reference_range values) and changes the pre-analysis UX (missing-range attention state). Both affect what data analysis receives and what the user is shown during review. Analytical scoring logic (report_compiler_v1.py, insight_graph, state_engine, signal evaluation) confirmed not in scope. Determinism preserved — range correction is explicit user action; same edited input produces same reference_range payload. No hidden fallback logic permitted (STOP 7). Three determinism risks surfaced: (1) one-sided bound type contract between frontend and normalize.py; (2) EditDialog spread vs explicit field overwrite; (3) type change to ParsedBiomarker.referenceRange min/max optionality.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:4 — HIGH. Stage 1C (lines 88-98) confirms HIGH: sprint changes governed user-supplied input entering analysis. No downgrade permitted.
automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:6 — MIXED: contract data shape changes (BEHAVIOUR) + UX attention layer (BEHAVIOUR) + governed input propagated to analysis (MIXED per SOP §4).

changes.check: 2A — backend/app/routes/upload.py exists; parser output fields confirmed
changes.citation: backend/app/routes/upload.py:128-145 — enriches biomarkers with SSOT metadata; does not modify range fields. Parser output carries ref_low (float|null), ref_high (float|null), referenceRange (raw string) — confirmed via llm_parser.py:333-342 and deterministic_parser.py:68-79.

changes.check: 2A — backend/app/routes/analysis.py exists; reference_range contract confirmed open
changes.citation: backend/app/routes/analysis.py:19-31 — AnalysisStartRequest.biomarkers: Dict[str, Any] — no reference_range field restriction. Line 130: normalize_biomarkers_with_metadata(request.biomarkers) called — confirmed to preserve reference_range per normalize.py:92-106.

changes.check: 2A — backend/core/canonical/normalize.py exists; one-sided bound support confirmed
changes.citation: backend/core/canonical/normalize.py:100-106 — constructs reference_range when (has_min OR has_max). Handles null min and null max separately. Both 'reference_range' (snake) and 'referenceRange' (camel) dict keys handled at lines 93-126. Backend will accept one-sided bounds correctly.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx exists; one-sided bound fidelity defect confirmed
changes.citation: frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx:224-229 — referenceRange built only when (b.ref_low != null && b.ref_high != null). One-sided bounds (e.g. only ref_high present) silently dropped; fallback to b.referenceRange which is a raw string ('') from LLM parser (llm_parser.py:338) — not a valid referenceRange object. Line 84-97: analysis payload correctly passes reference_range from biomarker.referenceRange if present.

frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx:82-87 — formData: { name, value, unit, status } — no referenceRange fields. Line 128-143: handleSave spreads ...biomarker (passively preserves existing referenceRange if set) but form renders no inputs for min/max/unit. User cannot add range to a biomarker that lacks one.

frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx:130-133 — renders referenceRange as 'min–max unit' or '—'. No per-row warning badge, icon, or attention indicator for biomarkers with missing or insufficient range data.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/types/parsed.ts exists; type requires assessment for one-sided bound support
changes.citation: frontend/app/types/parsed.ts:20 — referenceRange?: { min: number; max: number; unit: string }. Both min and max are required non-optional numbers. To store one-sided bounds (e.g. ref_high only), either min or max must become optional (?: number). Cursor must decide; change is bounded within upload-review seam. Backend normalize.py already accepts null min/max.

changes.check: 2A — No duplicate authority source for reference ranges
changes.citation: Single authority chain: lab parser (llm_parser.py) or user edit (EditDialog.tsx) → upload/page.tsx payload → analysis.py → normalize.py. No second SSOT range registry accessed. Consistent with STOP condition 4.

changes.check: 2B — Behavioural scope defined; Intelligence Core confirmed not touched
changes.citation: Prompt non-goals (lines 199-207): no changes to scoring policy, analytical logic, Gemini/narrative, results-page. sprint scope limited to: upload/page.tsx (parse mapping), EditDialog.tsx (add range fields), ParsedTable.tsx (attention UX), parsed.ts (type), state/upload.ts (if needed for range persistence). No pipeline, analytics, or ssot files in scope.

Prompt §Regression Targets (lines 254-284): existing valid range preserved, one-sided bounds not dropped, missing-range flag visible, edit flow saves min/max/unit and persists in payload, analysis-start reference_range contract not broken, no hidden fallback ranges introduced. All verifiable via targeted tests and browser walk-through.

STOP1: fixing upload-review mapping does not require scoring policy change. STOP2: manual range correction propagates via existing reference_range contract (analysis.py:89-96 already works). STOP3: preserving richer detail is a bounded frontend fix to upload/page.tsx:225. STOP4: no second authority introduced. STOP5: missing-range UX requires only per-row badge addition — no broad redesign. STOP6: apo ratio fix scoped to upload-review seam only. STOP7: no fallback range behaviour — manual correction is the only recovery path.

Risk 1: one-sided bound type contract — if min/max made optional in parsed.ts, Cursor must ensure normalize.py null-handling is tested (confirmed safe at normalize.py:100-106). Risk 2: EditDialog spread vs explicit field overwrite — must ensure new range fields in formData explicitly set referenceRange on save, not rely solely on spread from stale biomarker state. Risk 3: EditDialog formData initialisation — useEffect at EditDialog.tsx:90-99 must be extended to populate range fields when biomarker.referenceRange exists (avoids resetting user-entered data on re-render).

git branch --show-current returns 'main'. Prompt requires branch: feature/parse-range-edit-and-missing-range-ux. Cursor must checkout or create this branch before running run_work_package.py start. Not a hardening blocker.
